title: pyrrhocactus horridus
side_effects: Pyrrhocactus horridus can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering. In severe cases, improper use of the Pyrrhocactus horridus can lead to respiratory problems, such as difficulty breathing and bronchitis. Additionally, there is a risk of allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, which can be life-threatening if not treated promptly.
